title: Audit Log Maintenance
description: The system shall maintain an audit log that records all security-relevant events with precise timestamps, including event type, timestamp (with millisecond precision), user ID, source IP, and outcome.
rationale: To ensure compliance with security policies, monitor activities, investigate incidents, and maintain accountability for actions taken within the system.
business_value: Improves security posture by providing a comprehensive record of all relevant events, enabling timely detection and response to potential threats.

source.description: As a security administrator I need to maintain an audit log that records all security-relevant events with precise timestamps so that I can monitor, investigate, and ensure compliance with security policies.

acceptance_criteria: Event is recorded in the audit log
Log entry includes a precise timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ)
Event type, user ID, and outcome are captured
Log cannot be modified or deleted after creation
postconditions: All security-relevant events are recorded in the audit log with precise timestamps
Each log entry includes event type, user ID, source IP, and outcome
The log is append-only and tamper-proof
functional_constraints: Log must record events such as login attempts, access to sensitive data, permission changes, and failed actions
security_requirements: Logs should be searchable and exportable for analysis
Tamper-proof mechanism must guarantee the integrity of the log
edge_cases: What happens when the system is under heavy load and cannot keep up with log generation?
How does the system handle extremely high volume of events without compromising performance?
What happens if the system's clock drifts over time?
Can the log be modified or deleted by users with administrative privileges?
dependencies: The system must have a timestamping mechanism that can provide millisecond precision.
There must be an authentication and authorization system in place to identify users and their roles.
A database or storage solution is required to persist the audit logs.

interfaces.name: Event Notification API
interfaces.description: The security administrator uses this API to receive notifications about new events that have been recorded in the audit log.

interfaces.name: Audit Log Database
interfaces.description: This database stores all the security-relevant events with their timestamps, user IDs, source IPs, and outcomes.

test_cases.description: Test that a login attempt is correctly recorded in the audit log.
test_cases.expected_result: A log entry should be created with the event type as 'Login Attempt', precise timestamp, user ID, source IP, and outcome.

test_cases.description: Test that a tampering attempt on the audit log is detected.
test_cases.expected_result: The system should detect any modification or deletion of log entries and record this as an anomaly in its own logging mechanism.

test_cases.description: Test that the log can be searched and exported.
test_cases.expected_result: A user with appropriate permissions should be able to search for events based on various criteria (e.g., date range, user ID) and export the results in a standard format.
